items.title: Prefer stable CV winners
items.avoid: Selecting the highest mean CV metric when fold std is much larger than competitors.
items.prefer: Choose strong mean with low std; confirm with repeated CV when means are within noise.
items.why: Low spread indicates the model is not overfitting specific partition boundaries.
items.example: Ridge α=10 mean RMSE 0.41 std 0.03 beats α=0.01 mean 0.40 std 0.08 for deployment.

items.title: Nest when tuning aggressively
items.avoid: Publishing inner GridSearchCV best_score_ after searching dozens of configurations.
items.prefer: Report nested outer mean (and std) or evaluate once on a locked test set after inner selection.
items.why: Many inner trials on the same folds overfit the validation process.
items.example: Outer 5-fold × inner 3-fold GridSearchCV on SVM C and gamma; headline metric = outer mean only.

items.title: Keep test locked during CV tuning
items.avoid: Re-running grids on the test set because CV and test metrics disagree.
items.prefer: All model and hyperparameter decisions on train+val CV; single test evaluation after refit.
items.why: Test peeking overfits the final reported metric just like validation peeking.
items.example: Pick RF depth by stratified 5-fold on 90% dev; refit on 90%; score once on 10% test.

items.title: Refit after CV selection
items.avoid: Deploying the model trained on the last CV fold's training slice only.
items.prefer: After CV picks settings, refit on all development (train+val) data before test or production.
items.why: CV estimates generalization from partial trains; deployment should use all allowed development labels.
items.example: GridSearchCV(..., refit=True) on full dev X, y after inner CV picks alpha.
